Title: GOF launches task forces to curb petrol smuggling on Malaysia-Thailand border
Article:
TUMPAT: The General Operations Force (GOF) has set up four special task forces to strengthen Ops Tiris 3.0 and tackle petrol smuggling along the Malaysia-Thailand border.
GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the units were deployed to target syndicates involved in illegal movements of subsidised fuel.
"Besides petrol, the task forces will also focus on other subsidised goods," he said.
"These teams have been assigned specifically to combat fuel and subsidised goods smuggling along the Malaysia-Thailand border.
"They will carry out inspections, monitor petrol stations, and conduct patrols," he added.
He was speaking to reporters after a briefing on smuggling activities along the border in Pengkalan Kubor today.
Thailand is facing fuel shortages and panic buying as shipment delays linked to the Middle East conflict continue.
Radzi also said inspections have taken place at petrol stations, illegal jetties, storage facilities, and other identified transit points for smuggled goods.
"We have identified several hotspots being used as transit points, and enforcement will be intensified in these areas," he said.
Since March 20, GOF has conducted around 800 inspections under Ops Tiris, the highest number among all brigades, Radzi added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sebatik Island faces severe water shortage as dam nears collapse
Article:
TAWAU: Sebatik Island is now on the brink of a severe water crisis.
The island's only dam is reported to have enough supply for just about seven more days following prolonged dry weather and ongoing drought conditions.
The situation has not only raised concerns over water supply disruptions but also triggered complaints among residents who have long been affected by drought in the area.
A check at the dam today found that the current water level stands at just 0.9m, a sharp drop compared to its full capacity of 17m and the normal level of 5m, clearly indicating increasingly critical depletion.
Residents said they have been forced to take matters into their own hands by constructing tube wells as an alternative water source.
However, the quality of the water is not guaranteed and is only suitable for daily use such as washing clothes and bathing.
One resident, Shahban Rahman, 52, said he decided to build his own tube well after learning the method through YouTube, at a relatively low cost of around RM600.
However, he said the water from the source is not safe for drinking or cooking, forcing residents to purchase bottled mineral water, with monthly expenses reaching hundreds of ringgit due to logistical costs on the island.
Meanwhile, Kalabakan member of parliament Datuk Andy Muhammad Suryadi Bandi said immediate aid in the form of 3,500 cartons of 1.5-litre bottled mineral water has been distributed to affected residents.
He said inspections in several villages, including Kampung Bergosong, Sungai Tongkang, Sungai Laba and Wallace Bay, found that about 30 wells and tube wells built during the 2024 drought are still functioning but require regular maintenance to ensure water quality.
"The current status of water supply on Sebatik Island due to the drought since early February has yet to show signs of recovery.
"The dam's water level is now critically low, at less than one metre, even though it is the main source of water supply for residents here," he said after visiting the island today.
He said the Sabah State Water Department has implemented scheduled water rationing and is distributing treated clean water to households using water tankers.
"This rationing is necessary as there are concerns that if there is no rainfall in the near future, the dam may dry up completely, which is very worrying," he said.
At the same time, he said several immediate and long-term measures are being carried out to manage the situation, including distributing three cartons of mineral water to each affected household.
In addition, two units of portable water purification plants with a capacity of 1,000 litres per hour have been installed to process water from existing tube wells, while efforts are ongoing to obtain additional units to expand supply coverage.
He also said that a project to deepen and dredge the existing dam reservoir has been approved under the 13th Malaysia Plan and is currently at the procurement stage before implementation.
"Additionally, we have applied for state government funding for the construction of a new water reservoir and are currently awaiting approval," he said.
On March 25, the Tawau District Disaster Management Committee (JPBD) declared a drought disaster on Sebatik Island following prolonged dry weather conditions.
However, Tawau Municipal Council president and JPBD chairman Datuk Joseph Pang later cancelled and withdrew the declaration about two hours after it was issued, as it no longer met the required criteria based on the assessment and monitoring by the Sabah State Disaster Management Committee (JPBN).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Women turn hot spell into profitable sun-dried fish business
Article:
PONTIAN: The prolonged dry spell has proven a boon for the fishing community in Kampung Nelayan Pontian Besar Kiri, who are taking advantage of the intense heat to produce sun-dried fish.
Entrepreneur Asmah Maiden, 66, has been in the trade for 40 years, supplementing her family's income, particularly when her children were still in school.
"The life of a fisherman is often unpredictable, especially during the monsoon season when rough seas prevent them from heading out," she said.
Assisted by one worker, Asmah's husband, Mohd Faizal Abdullah, 76, used to be a fisherman but had to stop six years ago due to a nerve condition.
Despite using traditional methods, she ensures her products meet quality standards before they reach the market.
The mother of five sources a variety of fish species, including gelama, duri, kembung, and selar kuning, from local fishermen, crediting the community for the longevity of her business.
"Thanks to the fishermen here who provide me with stock at reasonable prices, I can maintain affordable rates for wholesalers and customers from both within and outside the district," she said.
"I can dry up to 60kg of fish at a time. Prices vary by type, with gelama, for instance, sold at RM15 per kg. Hot weather is crucial for us, affecting both those at sea and those of us in processing," she added.
Her employee, Fatimah Payang, 55, said several other fishermen's wives had also taken up the craft to provide supplementary household income.
"It helps me support my husband, Ali Munir Sharif, 60, who stopped fishing 25 years ago after losing his right leg in a road accident. Alhamdulillah, the hot weather produces higher quality dried fish and directly boosts our earnings," she said.
Regular customer Kak Nor, 70, praised the women's industriousness.
"This not only keeps them occupied but sets an example for the younger generation. The fish here is high quality and reasonably priced," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man detained after erratic public behaviour disrupts traffic
Article:
IPOH: Police have detained a man who was behaving erratically and disrupting traffic in Langkap, near Teluk Intan, yesterday.
Hilir Perak police chief Assistant Commissioner Chua Kok Lian said a report was received at 9.35pm regarding a naked man acting indecently and obstructing traffic along Jalan Langkap–Chui Chak.
"Acting on the information, police detained the 41-year-old man, as seen in videos circulating on social media.
"The suspect was taken to Langkap police station for further action before being referred to Teluk Intan Hospital for treatment and a psychiatric evaluation," he said in a statement today.
Chua also urged the public not to share videos of the incident, to protect the individual's dignity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Umno branch meetings set tone ahead of Johor polls
Article:
PONTIAN: The 2,380 Johor Umno branches currently holding their annual meetings may be doing so for the last time before the next state election.
The branch meetings, which involve the Women, Youth and Puteri wings, began on April 1 and are scheduled to run until May 17.
Johor Umno liaison committee deputy chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan said the current Barisan Nasional (BN) state government's mandate is expected to end in April 2027.
As Umno branch meetings typically begin in April each year, this session will likely be the final one before the Johor State Legislative Assembly is dissolved, he said.
Ahmad, who is also the Pontian member of parliament, was speaking after attending an akikah ceremony at Kampung Parit Dahlan here today.
He said the meetings provide a platform for branch chiefs to brief members on the state government's achievements.
Various incentives implemented by the state government, such as Bantuan Kasih Johor, must be explained to members so they understand the efforts being made for the people's welfare, he said.
Ahmad, who is also an Umno Supreme Council member, said branch leaders should highlight Johor's successes, including high investment levels, lower unemployment rates and increased job opportunities.
He added that the meetings should emphasise the state's stability and social harmony.
The Johor Maju 2030 concept, which aims to make Johor a developed state within four years, must be a key agenda in the winding-up speeches of branch chiefs, he said.
He said they must also remind members to prepare for the state election by organising their machinery now to avoid any weaknesses.
In the 2022 Johor state election, BN won 40 of the 56 seats contested, leading to the formation of the state government under Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: JB cops searching for Rohingya girl, 11, missing since March 20
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Police have launched a search for an 11-year-old Rohingya girl who has been missing for more than two weeks ago.
Johor Baru (North) police chief Assistant Commissioner Radin Ramlan Radin Taha said efforts were ongoing to trace Yasmin Kefayatullah, who was last seen on March 20.
He said the girl was last seen at her home in No. 502, Jalan Kupang 2, Kampung Usaha Jaya, Skudai Kiri, here, about 1.30pm.
"Police are working to locate the missing child and are appealing to members of the public who may have information to come forward," he said today.
Yasmin is 95cm tall, weighs 28kg, has a dark complexion, slim build and long straight black hair.
She was last seen wearing a T-shirt and pink trousers.
People with information on her whereabouts should contact investigating officer Inspector Mun Nyanggau at 019-257 4051, the district police hotline at 07-556 1222 or go to the nearest police station.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Communications Ministry sec-gen visits media personnel at Perak Madani Rakyat Programme
Article:
IPOH: Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah today spent time with media personnel at the Media Centre of the Perak edition of the Madani Rakyat Programme (PMR) 2026, currently taking place at Bulatan Sultan Azlan Shah, Ipoh.
Also present were Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) managing director Abdul Karim Fakir Ali, MCMC chief officer of state affairs Bukhari Yahya, Perak MCMC director Mohd Irwan Mohamad, and Perak Information Department director Azrul Hassan.
He met about 40 media practitioners covering the three-day programme, which began yesterday, and presented them with tokens of appreciation.
Earlier, upon arriving at 11am, he visited booths of participating agencies, including the Malaysian National News Agency (Bernama) booth, to observe the services and initiatives offered to the public.
Perak, which kicks off PMR 2026, is targeting about 100,000 visitors by its second day today, offering more than 200 government services as well as various engaging programmes.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to officiate the programme today. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Blood clots, burning eyes: Pollution chokes north Thailand
Article:
AFTER hours spent in the thick pollution-choking parts of northern Thailand, Pon Doikam gets home and blows her burning nose to find blood clots spattered across the tissue.
"It's suffocating," the 36-year-old coconut seller told AFP in Chiang Mai, a tourist destination among the areas affected by dangerous pollution levels this week.
"It feels like you're trapped in the smoke constantly."
Seasonal agricultural burning, forest fires and weather patterns produce an annual pollution season across much of Southeast Asia.
But parts of northern Thailand are seeing haze that even hardened locals say is exceptional.
"I've lived in Chiang Mai since I was a kid, and this is the worst it has ever been," said Pon, who works outdoors all day.
"I don't have a choice," she added. "I have to come out, making a living day to day."
This week Thailand's second city, Chiang Mai, regularly topped the IQAir monitor website's most polluted big cities list.
The situation is even worse to the west in Pai, a backpacker destination known for its greenery and mountains.
Some monitors there recorded levels of PM2.5 – particles small enough to enter the bloodstream and linked to diseases like cancer – of over 900 microgrammes per cubic metre.
That is 60 times higher than the World Health Organisation's recommended 24-hour average exposure.
The area's mountainous geography makes it doubly vulnerable. Smoke is easily trapped, and the forested hillsides are hard to access when fires start.
AFP saw multiple blazes burning on hillsides and along roads between Pai and Chiang Mai, lighting up patches of otherwise pitch-black countryside.
Volunteer firefighters like Maitree Nuanja do their best to bolster limited local capacity, relying heavily on donations such as drinking water and face masks.
"The fire control centre gave us 20 litres of fuel and lent us leaf blowers. Once the season ends, we have to return them," he told AFP, standing before a blackened, ash-strewn stretch of land.
"Everyone can see how serious it is now. It's so dark and hazy you can't see a thing, and it's gone on for far too long."
He worries about his home next to a forest, and his health.
"We now live with this smoke, breathing it in every day."
On Wednesday, a volunteer firefighter was found dead from suspected exhaustion linked to heat and underlying health conditions, officials told AFP.
Pollution is particularly dangerous to those with existing health problems, the elderly and children.
[[nid:1410256]]
In Chiang Mai, the government has installed hundreds of "dust-free rooms" – equipped with air purifiers and positive pressure systems that keep out the polluted air – including at the retirement facility that Watwilai Chaiwan now calls home.
The retired nurse, 82, said the pollution made her afraid to go out and aggravated her dizziness and migraines.
"It's a real problem for the elderly. You have to wear a mask the entire time you're breathing this air," she told AFP.
Thailand's government held talks this week on the haze, and some districts in Chiang Mai have issued disaster declarations to help speed up financial support.
But clean air activists say more is needed, and are pushing for the government to move quickly on clean air legislation that stalled last year with the dissolution of parliament.
"A normal government would have been concerned about clean air not only now but a long time ago," said Kanongnij Sribuaiam, legal team leader at Thailand Clean Air Network, which pushed for the legislation.
If no action is taken by May 13, the legislation will expire and the process will have to start from scratch, she added.
"The public is very disappointed."
In Chiang Mai, doctor Thanakrit Im-iam was wearing a heavy-duty respirator to protect himself.
He warned that the long-term health consequences of the pollution are "devastating."
"It affects everyone because these toxins and heavy metals enter the body directly," he told AFP, describing "burning eyes, phlegm, and nasal inflammation."
The mask is his only protection, he said.
"We can't control the rest. That's up to the government."
The pollution is also increasingly a deterrent for tourists, who form a key pillar of the region's economy.
"Usually, Chiang Mai is buzzing in March and April, but this year, it's just quiet," said tuk-tuk driver Chakkrawat Wichitchaisilp.
At a viewpoint overlooking Chiang Mai, the city skyline was almost entirely obscured, with only faint outlines of hills visible through a dense grey haze and a dim orange sun hanging in the sky.
"I've taken photos from this very spot, and it's just a beautiful blue sky – and you can see as far as the horizon," said Martin Astill, 57, a Briton who previously lived in Thailand and was visiting with his family.
"This is shocking. Never seen it as bad as this."
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar to make maiden Pengerang visit as PM, engaging felda settlers
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will make his inaugural visit to Pengerang tomorrow, marking his first official engagement in the area since taking office.
The visit will commence at Dewan Semai Bakti, Felda Semenchu, with a programme involving Felda settlers.
Anwar is scheduled to deliver a keynote address and engage directly with the community.
He is also expected to present contributions to asnaf recipients and take part in a meet-and-greet session with the settlers.
Johor Agriculture, Agro-based Industry and Rural Development Committee chairman Datuk Zahari Sarip will represent Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi at the event.
Following the Felda programme, the prime minister will head to SMK Tanjung Pengelih in Pengerang to officially launch the upgrading of the school into Kolej Vokasional Pengerang.
The initiative aims to bolster technical and vocational education and training (TVET) in the region, supporting skills development and employability among youths.
Organised by the Pengerang parliamentary service centre, the two-part visit is expected to draw local leaders and residents, reinforcing Putrajaya's ongoing focus on rural engagement and educational development.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fuel smugglers enlarge tanks to evade detection, says Armizan
Article:
TUMPAT: Petrol smugglers are adapting their methods, including modifying vehicle tanks to increase capacity for illicit petrol.
Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said syndicates have enlarged vehicle tanks to store subsidised Malaysian petrol.
The petrol is then smuggled into Thailand, where prices are much higher, making the practice highly lucrative.
"Smugglers are constantly changing their tactics to evade detection by enforcement authorities," he said.
He spoke during a visit to the General Operations Force (GOF) Tactical Base today.
Also present were the ministry's enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam and GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain.
During the visit, Armizan and Azman inspected two lorries and two cars seized in a recent joint operation by the state KPDN and GOF.
The vehicles, modified with enlarged tanks, are believed to have been used in petrol smuggling.
Armizan said such modifications allow syndicates to maximise petrol collection in a single trip before transporting it illegally across the border.
It is reported that, besides lorries, smugglers are using cars such as Proton Wira and Proton Iswara to move Malaysian petrol into Thailand.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Media veterans recall Dr Ling's warmth, leadership and lasting legacy
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Behind his role as a long-serving minister, Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik was a leader known for his warmth and openness, even welcoming members of the media into his home, recalled Malaysian National News Agency (Bernama) Chairman Datuk Seri Wong Chun Wai.
Wong, a prominent media figure and National Journalism Laureate who covered Dr Ling for decades, described the former MCA president as a "soft-spoken but firm" politician who maintained a close relationship with the press corps throughout his career.
"Despite holding many senior positions in politics, he opened his home to reporters who covered him. I was lucky to see him up close and personal," he told Bernama today.
Dr
Ling died today
aged 83,  leaving behind his wife, Toh Puan Ong Ee Nah, and their two sons, Ling Hee Leong and Ling Hee Keat.
He was the longest-serving president in MCA's history, leading the party for 17 years.
Wong, who also served in various positions at
The Star
after beginning his career as a junior reporter in Penang in 1984, fondly remembered Dr Ling as a warm and down-to-earth leader who showed a more personal side beyond his role in government, including his love for singing, especially his favourite 1950s hit 'Whatever Will Be, Will Be (Que Sera, Sera)'.
He said that while the former minister was known for his personal charm, Dr Ling was equally tireless in his duties, frequently criss-crossing the country to meet grassroots members and championing the establishment of Universiti Tunku Abdul Rahman (UTAR).
"He played a major role in the transformation of Malaysia, from overseeing the construction of Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to positioning Port Klang in Selangor as a global player, yet he never lost that personal touch," Wong added.
Meanwhile, the Editor's Association of Chinese Medium of Malaysia President Datuk Kuik Cheng Kang also described the late Dr Ling as a calm and soft-spoken leader whose measured approach reflected both his character and leadership style.
"He never spoke in a rush and was always calm and measured, delivering his words one sentence at a time, compelling anyone listening to focus fully," said Kuik, recalling his experience covering Dr Ling as a journalist.
Kuik, who is also a board member of the Malaysia Media Council, said Dr Ling played a pivotal role in strengthening Tunku Abdul Rahman University of Management and Technology (TAR UMT) and was widely regarded as the institution's "father".
"He never allowed politics to enter the campus, and as a result, TAR UMT has always remained true to its mission of providing quality education for Chinese youths," he said.
Kuik added that Dr Ling's dedication to the Chinese community was evident through his disciplined routine and active engagement with other leaders, including former Gerakan president, the late Tun Dr Lim Keng Yaik, and former MIC president, the late Tun S. Samy Vellu, in addressing key issues.
Meanwhile, MCA, in a statement today, said the party will always remember Dr Ling's enormous contributions to the party, the nation and the country.
"His unwavering spirit, calm judgment, and far-sighted strategies will continue to serve as a model for all party leaders and members, and will always be a guiding light for our party's progress," the statement read.
It said party president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong has instructed the MCA headquarters to fly its flag at half-mast as a sign of mourning.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia's Mount Dukono erupts, sends ash 1,400m high
Article:
JAKARTA: Mount Dukono in eastern Indonesia erupted on Saturday, sending an ash column up to 1,400m above its summit.
An officer at the Dukono Volcano Observation Post (PGA), Bambang Sugiono, said the eruption occurred at approximately 11.08am local time.
"The ash column was observed to be white to grey in colour, with thick intensity, drifting towards the southwest following wind direction," he said according to ANTARA News Agency, on Saturday.
He said Mount Dukono, which rises 1,087m above sea level, remains at Alert Level II (Waspada), indicating above-normal volcanic activity.
Seismographs records also showed ongoing and periodic volcanic activity.
Bambang warned that eruptions at Mount Dukono are highly fluctuating and may occur at any time, with ash distribution depending on changing wind direction and speed.
He also cautioned that volcanic ash poses health risks, particularly to the respiratory system, urging people in affected areas to wear masks or face coverings.
Mount Dukono erupted twice on Friday, with the first eruption at 11.06am local time, producing an ash column reaching approximately 1,500m above the summit, and a later eruption at 5.28pm, with ash columns as high as 4,000m above the summit.
– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Suspected panther attack sparks fear among Gua Musang residents
Article:
GUA MUSANG: About 30 families living in the Gua Musang Hospital quarters fear for their safety after the carcass of a sheep, believed to have been attacked by a black panther, was found just 10m from their homes on Thursday.
Resident Mohd Helmiey Shudi Hussin, 42, said he had lived in the quarters for six years and had never encountered such an incident before.
"The attack occurred very close to our homes, raising serious concerns for our safety, especially for children who often play outside.
"I understand that some long-time residents had previously encountered a panther in the quarters area, but it was captured a few years ago," he said when met by reporters here today.
Helmiey urged the authorities to take immediate action, adding that the quarters were located near a cave, which may serve as a habitat for the wild animal.
Meanwhile, Kelantan Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) director Wan Mohd Adib Wan Yusoh said it would gather further information on the incident.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Senegal bans government travel as Iran war oil shock hits public finances
Article:
DAKAR: Senegal's government has suspended all non-essential foreign travel by ministers and top officials, warning of "extremely difficult" times ahead as the US-Israeli conflict with Iran drives global oil prices higher, straining the nation's budget.
The war and Iran's effective closure of the vital Strait of Hormuz have roiled global energy markets, sending the price of benchmark Brent crude soaring and pushing governments around the world to take steps to mitigate the negative impacts.
Addressing a youth event in the coastal town of Mbour on Friday evening, Senegal's Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko pointed to oil trading at about US$115 a barrel, nearly twice the US$62 per barrel assumed in Senegal's budget projections.
"No minister in my government will leave the country unless it is for an essential mission related to the work we are currently undertaking," he said, announcing that he had already cancelled his own planned trips to Niger, Spain and France.
Governments across the West African region and globally have scrambled to respond to the crisis with measures including fuel price increases, subsidies, and remote work.
Sonko cited such measures as justification for debt-laden Senegal's own steps.
He said additional measures would be announced next week, with the Energy and Mines Minister expected to address the nation in the coming days to detail efforts to mitigate the impact of the price shock.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Minor 3.1-magnitude quake hits off Batu Pahat coast
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A minor 3.1-magnitude earthquake struck off the coast of Batu Pahat this afternoon.
The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said the tremor occurred at 1.54pm, about 42km south of Batu Pahat, at a depth of 10km.
Tremors may have been felt across parts of Johor. MetMalaysia said it would continue monitoring the situation.
MetMalaysia has requested residents who felt the tremors to provide feedback by completing a survey at https://forms.gle/CyUwHQE9LnyuWaJ37.
Today's earthquake is the
fourth recorded
in the district.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 60 top SPM students in Kedah offered trip to Japan, Indonesia
Article:
KUALA NERANG: Sixty students in Padang Terap who scored excellent results in the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) will be rewarded with educational trips to Japan and Indonesia.
The trips are offered by Non-Governmental Organisation (NGO) Kedah Anak Muda Madani.
Its chairman, Datuk Zuraidi Rahim, said the students were from two schools adopted by the NGO, as well as seven other schools in the district.
"For students from the adopted schools, I will take 20 of them on an educational visit to Japan, while 40 excellent students from the other schools will be taken to Jakarta, Indonesia," he said.
"Originally, I planned to take those from the adopted schools to London, but due to the conflict in the Middle East, I have discussed with the Kedah State Education Department and the District Education Office to change the destination to Japan," he told reporters here today.
Zuraidi said the trips are planned for next month (May) before the students continue their studies at higher learning institutions.
He said that the educational visits are not just for the students' enjoyment, but will also be filled with educational and scholarly content, including visits to higher learning institutions in both countries involved.
"In Indonesia, students will visit several universities recognised by the government, especially in the field of medicine, to inspire them.
"In Japan, students will be taken to visit cities such as Tokyo and Osaka to see for themselves the learning culture and progress in that country (Japan)," he said.
According to Zuraidi, his team hopes the initiative will inspire the students involved to continue excelling in their education to achieve their ambitions.
In addition, he said, students in Padang Terap who excelled in the Sijil Tinggi Pelajaran Malaysia (STPM) examination and obtained a Cumulative Grade Point Average (CGPA) of 4.0 would be taken on a trip to Bali, Indonesia. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Raid exposes illegal settlements with surau, shop and subsidised goods in Setia Alam
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A total of 214 undocumented migrants were found living in three illegal settlements concealed within an oil palm plantation behind a hilly area in Setia Alam here.
The settlements were uncovered during a midnight Op Bersepadu Mega conducted by the Immigration Department, with the assistance of police and several other enforcement agencies.
Reporters accompanying the operation found that the raiding team had to traverse hilly terrain for about 400m before reaching the site.
Officers not only had to contend with the dark and rugged surroundings but also the risk of encountering venomous animals as they moved in to apprehend the migrants.
A survey of the area revealed that despite its remote location, the settlement was equipped with various facilities, including a surau, a grocery shop, and access to subsidised items such as gas cylinders and diesel. It also had electricity and water supply.
Several chicken coops were spotted, believed to be linked to cockfighting activities, while escape routes were detected around the area, thought to have been used by occupants to flee during enforcement operations.
Selangor Immigration director Khairrul Aminus Kamaruddin said the three settlements were believed to have existed for between five and nine years.
He said 356 foreigners were screened during the operation, with 214 detained for suspected immigration offences.
"Those detained comprised 120 men, 65 women, 16 boys and 13 girls, aged between one month and 80 years.
"Initial checks found that they were nationals of Indonesia, Myanmar, Bangladesh, India, Pakistan and Sri Lanka," he said when met at the scene today.
Khairrul Aminus said most of those detained worked as labourers and cleaners in the surrounding area.
He also did not rule out the possibility that the settlement had a "caretaker" acting as a leader who collected monthly rent.
The operation was carried out with the assistance of Immigration Departments from Putrajaya, Kuala Lumpur, Negri Sembilan and Penang, as well as the Shah Alam City Council, the General Operations Force, the National Registration Department and the Civil Defence Force.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor eyes 1,000 high-skilled jobs from RM700mil China investment
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor is targeting more than 1,000 high-skilled jobs under a proposed RM700 million expansion by China-based GSP Automotive Group Wenzhou Co Ltd.
Menteri Besar Johor Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the potential investment, expected to materialise by 2030, would anchor job opportunities in high-technology manufacturing, including battery systems and robotics.
"Johor continues to attract high-quality investments through strategic engagements such as this with GSP Automotive Group.
"The proposed expansion, valued at approximately RM700 million, is expected to create more than 1,000 high-skilled job opportunities by 2030, particularly in advanced manufacturing and TVET-related sectors.
"The state government is firm that every job created must prioritise 'anak Johor' (Johor-born).
"Our focus is on holistic talent development through structured training programmes, strategic collaboration with Kumpulan Prasarana Rakyat Johor Sdn Bhd, and coordination by the Johor Talent Development Council to ensure clear, high-quality career pathways.
"This approach is about facilitating investments while ensuring that employment opportunities, skills development, and economic benefits are directly felt by the people of Johor," he said in a statement today.
GSP, a leading player in automotive parts manufacturing, is expanding into battery technology and robotics segments aligned with shifting industry demands for automation and electrification.
The discussions also covered workforce requirements, streamlined approval processes, and enhanced industry support through automation incentives and grants under the Invest Malaysia Facilitation Centre-Johor framework.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No food price hikes while reserves last, says Mat Sabu
Article:
MELAKA: The government says food prices will remain stable as long as imported and domestic supplies are sufficient.
Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said the country's food supply is stable and not facing shortages.
Mohamad, popularly known as Mat Sabu, said price stability depends on existing national reserves.
"Prices will hold while old stock lasts, but may rise when new supplies come in at higher prices."
"The impact depends on the situation – I cannot guarantee it for a long period.
"When reserve stock runs out and we have to use new stock at new prices, then price changes may occur," he said after officiating the Sentuhan Agro Madani Programme at Dewan Datuk Demang Hussin in Bukit Katil here today.
Also present were Farmers' Organisation Authority (LPP) chairman Datuk Mahfuz Omar and Hang Tuah Jaya member of parliament Adam Adli.
Mohamad, who is also Kota Raja MP, said the situation remains under control and the government is on standby to manage any eventuality.
"The prime minister (Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim) can call on us at any time to discuss and issue directives if necessary.
"Relevant agencies, including the LPP, will continue to channel information to farmers on policy changes or developments.
"LPP and other agencies will provide information directly to farmers if any changes occur," he said.
On Sunday, the Federation of Malaysian Hawkers and Traders Associations said food prices are expected to rise by up to 50 per cent following the recent increase in fuel costs.
Its president Datuk Seri Rosli Sulaiman said the increase is due to higher costs of raw materials and basic goods, as transportation and delivery rely largely on diesel vehicles.
On Friday, the United Nations Food and Agriculture Organisation (FAO) said world food prices rose in March to their highest level since September last year and could climb further if the Middle East conflict that pushed up energy prices continues.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Search continues for 3 feared drowned in Pahang
Article:
KUANTAN: The search and rescue (SAR) operation for three people feared drowned in separate incidents at Pantai Sepat here and Kampung Durian Hijau in Jerantut is continuing today.
Pahang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Mohd Salahuddin Isa said the search for fisherman Mohd Ishak Ismail, 63, who went missing at Pantai Sepat, has entered its third day.
He said Mohd Ishak left the Tanjung Api jetty around 10 am on Wednesday and would normally return the following day to the same jetty, but failed to do so.
A missing person report was later lodged on Thursday.
Meanwhile, Mohd Salahuddin said efforts to locate Muhammad Amirul Ashraf Abdullah and Muhammad Danial Haikal Shamsudin, both aged 14, who went missing while bathing in Sungai Pahang at Kampung Durian Hijau, have also been intensified.
"The Water Rescue Team has conducted dives at the location where the victims are believed to have fallen, as well as along several downstream areas.
"Three personnel from the Kuala Krau Fire and Rescue Station are also carrying out surface searches," he said in a statement.
– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dzulkefly: Private hospitals pledge support for MHIT base plan
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Private hospitals have pledged support for the voluntary Malaysia Health and Insurance/Takaful (MHIT) base plan, set to launch in January 2027.
Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said it is a whole-of-nation effort to improve affordability and access in private healthcare.
"At a meeting convened by the Minister of Health and Minister of Finance II in Putrajaya yesterday, private hospitals expressed their support for the base MHIT plan," he told the New Straits Times.
A small scale operational pilot will commence in stages, focused in the Greater Klang Valley area, from July 2026, he said.
Dzulkefly said Diagnosis Related Groups (DRGs) will be implemented in phases as part of this initiative, to improve transparency, predictability, and to move toward value-based healthcare in an orderly manner.
"Digital health is a critical enabler. We are ready to connect our public and private systems under a data governance framework that keeps individuals in control of their data," he said in a social media post.
The gathering held at the Finance Ministry was attended by Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan, 14 private hospital CEOs, ministry officials, and Bank Negara representatives
Dzulkefly also said such interoperability will reduce the duplication of medical imaging and tests, thus saving costs while improving the continuity of care.
"We thank the private hospital CEOs for their commitment to provide the data needed to phase in DRGs for payments from January 2027," he added.
Dzulkefly said that the meeting "went well", with the government responsive to industry concerns, including licensing and regulations.
"Medical inflation is a pressing issue. Instead of harmful short term knee jerk reaction, we have to take a comprehensive approach towards value based healthcare," he said.
In February, Dzulkefly warned that the government is pressing ahead with
healthcare reforms
to tackle rising medical costs, growing insurance dropouts, and mounting pressure on public hospitals.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Traders report stable vegetable prices despite cost pressures
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Vegetable prices at public markets around the city remain stable, with no significant hikes despite the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.
A check at the Taman Suria public market found that vegetable supplies are sufficient, even as geopolitical uncertainties raise concerns over potential supply chain disruptions.
Traders said prices from farm suppliers remain reasonable despite rising operating costs following the diesel price hike.
Most of them source their produce from local suppliers, which has helped maintain price stability.
Vegetables such as cabbage from Cameron Highlands showed no significant price changes, although the price of spring onions has increased by about RM10 per kg.
Trader Samri Kemat, 63, said most vegetable prices have remained steady, with no signs of a sudden spike.
Prices remain stable for red and green chillies, which are sold at RM11 to RM12 per kg, Japanese mustard greens (sawi Jepun) at RM8 per kg, water spinach (kangkung) at RM6 per kg, and mustard greens (sawi) at around RM8 per kg, he said.
He said cabbage is still sold at RM8 per kg, round spinach at RM6.60 per kg, and tomatoes at about RM2 per kg.
Prices are currently the same as they were before the Middle East conflict.
"We do not know what will happen in the future," he said when met today.
Another trader, Mazlina Mustafa, 53, said that while most prices are stable, spring onions saw a price hike before the recent Raya celebrations.
She said the price of spring onions from Cameron Highlands suppliers rose from between RM6 and RM7 per kg to RM17 per kg at the wholesale level.
"I used to sell them at RM8 to RM10 per kg, but now I have to sell them at about RM20 per kg because the supply price has increased," she said.
"Different traders have different suppliers. My spring onion prices might have gone up slightly, while others may not have increased theirs," she said, adding that the hike only affected spring onions while other vegetables remained at normal prices.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor allocates RM1.85mil to support Sikh community
Article:
PONTIAN: The Johor government has announced a RM1.85 million allocation to support Sikh institutions and community development across the state.
Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the funding would go towards education, infrastructure and welfare initiatives, bringing total state contributions to the Sikh community to nearly RM5 million since 2022.
The announcement was made during a Vaisakhi luncheon at Gurdwara Sahib Pontian, which also marked the temple's 50th anniversary and drew Sikh organisations and community leaders from across Johor.
Onn Hafiz, who attended as guest of honour, described the Sikh community as a pillar of service and unity, highlighting its tradition of langgar, or community kitchens that serve all regardless of race or religion.
He also noted the community's humanitarian efforts during recent floods.
"I am truly touched by this gesture, which exemplifies and strengthens the Bangsa Johor concept," he said.
Under the allocation, RM200,000 each will be channelled to Johor Punjabi School and Gurdwara Sahib Johor Baru, while six other gurdwaras in Kluang, Batu Pahat, Muar, Pontian and Segamat will receive RM100,000 each.
Additional funding includes RM200,000 for the Johor Sikhs Sports Club, and RM100,000 each for the Malaysian Sikh Union (Johor branch) and Johor Sikh Association.
A further RM550,000 has been earmarked for building works, including RM300,000 for Gurdwara Sahib Pontian and RM250,000 for the Johor Baru gurdwara.
Representatives of the Sikh community expressed appreciation to the Johor royal household, particularly His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, and Regent Tunku Mahkota Ismail, for fostering inclusivity and harmony in the state.
They said the Bangsa Johor vision has ensured all communities are valued and able to contribute meaningfully to Johor's development.
The event was also attended by Pontian MP Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan, who sponsored the luncheon.
Organisers said the state-level Vaisakhi celebration would become an annual event, with next year's gathering set to be held in Kluang.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China raises concerns over Israel's death penalty law targeting Palestinians
Article:
BEIJING: China called for Palestinians' rights to be protected after Israel approved a bill to allow the execution of Palestinians convicted on terror charges for deadly attacks.
Under the new law, passed by Israel's parliament on Monday, Palestinians in the occupied West Bank convicted by military courts of carrying out deadly attacks classified as "terrorism" will face the death penalty as a default sentence.
"The legal rights of the Palestinian people should be respected and protected," China's foreign ministry spokeswoman Mao Ning said when asked about the bill at a press briefing.
"We also hope relevant parties will cease actions that escalate tensions and exacerbate conflict," she said, without mentioning Israel by name.
"China believes that any law should fulfil legal principles such as equality and justice and should not discriminate on the basis of ethnicity, religion or nationality or political views," Mao added.
A host of countries have criticised the bill, which was supported by Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
The United Nations said on Tuesday that applying the new bill in occupied Palestinian territory would constitute a war crime.
The foreign ministers of Saudi Arabia, Egypt, Jordan, Indonesia, Pakistan, Qatar, Turkiye and the United Arab Emirates also lambasted the bill in a joint statement on Thursday.
"This legislation constitutes a dangerous escalation, particularly given its discriminatory application against Palestinian prisoners, and stressed that such measures risk further exacerbating tensions and undermining regional stability," the statement read.
The European Union also criticised the bill, but the United States has come out in support of "Israel's sovereign right to determine its own laws."
China still uses the death penalty and does not release statistics on executions.
Amnesty International and other rights groups believe thousands of people are executed in the country every year.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Melaka takes WRUR to parliament level, starts in Alor Gajah
Article:
JASIN: The Melaka government is strengthening the
Wakil Rakyat Untuk Rakyat
(WRUR) programme by expanding it to the parliamentary level, beginning in Alor Gajah this year to enhance service delivery.
Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh said the move aims to ensure the presence of elected representatives and the government is felt at the grassroots, while enabling faster resolution of local issues.
He said the Asahan state constituency marks the starting point of this year's WRUR rollout, which adopts a new approach extended to the parliamentary level through a series of programmes over about a month.
"The programme begins in Asahan from April 4 to 11, followed by Gadek (April 12–18) and Machap Jaya (April 19 - 25)," he told reporters after attending the Melaka Sayang Rakyat Aidilfitri celebration here today.
Earlier, he launched the WRUR programme, which Asahan assemblyman Datuk Fairul Nizam Roslan also attended.
Ab Rauf said 11 WRUR programmes have been implemented so far out of 28 state constituencies in Melaka, with nearly 90 per cent of public complaints resolved, while the rest are being addressed.
He said among the key achievements in Asahan is the completion of the RM11 million second Jus Water Treatment Plant, which is expected to begin operations in May and benefit nearly 25,000 users.
The programme has also involved more than 500 Orang Asli through 10 special initiatives to ensure inclusivity in development.
Over the past three years, more than 200 initiatives have been realised, including nearly RM23.9 million in welfare aid disbursed to residents in Asahan alone, he added.
Meanwhile, Ab Rauf said the state does not see a need to implement a work-from-home policy for now, citing Melaka's small geographical size and good accessibility.
"Melaka has good connectivity and short travel distances, so working from home is not a pressing need at this time," he said. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: King, Queen extend condolences over Liong Sik's passing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, King and Queen of Malaysia, gave their condolences over the passing of former MCA president and transport minister Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik today.
Their message was shared via the official Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar.
Earlier, MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong
confirmed
the news of his passing this morning.
In a tribute, Wee said that Dr Ling will be remembered not only for his statesmanship and vision but also for his integrity, dedication, and deep love for the country.
"Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik was not merely a leader; he was a nation builder, a unifier, and a visionary whose legacy will live on through the institutions he built, the policies he shaped and the lives he touched."
Other political leaders have also
shared tributes
in honour of Dr Ling.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil shared his condolences to the family of the late former Mata Kuching and Labis member of parliament, whom he described as a highly respected political figure who made significant contributions to the nation.
Former communications and multimedia minister Tan Sri Annuar Musa in a social media post said: "Tun Ling Liong Sik was my senior at the Royal Military College, a colleague in Tun Dr Mahathir's cabinet and my neighbour in Bukit Damansara.
"His passing is a tremendous loss for the nation. My family and I deeply feel the loss of a dear friend and close neighbour.
"May Toh Puan and the family find strength and courage in facing this very sad moment."
Meanwhile, the MCA, on its official Facebook page, said: "All MCA colleagues are deeply saddened and express our deepest and sincere condolences to the family of Tun Dr Ling."
Wee earlier instructed that all party flags at MCA premises nationwide be flown at half-mast, calling it a mark of
MCA's deepest respect
.
Born on Sept 18, 1943, in Kuala Kangsar, Perak, Dr Ling received his primary education at King Edward VII School in Taiping and his secondary education at the Royal Military College in Port Dickson, Negri Sembilan.
He then studied medicine at the University of Singapore and served as a doctor at Penang General Hospital.
Dr Ling began his political career in 1968 when he joined the MCA. In 1974, he was elected as the member of Parliament for Mata Kuching (now Bagan) in Penang and retained the seat for two terms before moving to the Labis parliamentary seat in Johor in 1986, which he held until 2004.
In 1976, he was appointed Parliamentary Secretary to the Local Government and Federal Territories Ministry, followed by roles as deputy information minister, deputy finance minister, and deputy education minister.
He served as transport minister from Jan 7, 1986, until his retirement on May 23, 2003.
Dr Ling was elected as MCA's sixth president in 1986 and held the position until 2003.
He was installed as University Tunku Abdul Rahman's (Utar) first chancellor during the university's 24th convocation ceremony on Jan 18, 2017.
In 2015, then-prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak filed a defamation suit against Ling, alleging he implied Najib had misused public funds for personal gain.
Najib later withdrew the suit in 2018.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: WHO urges urgent health support in Middle East conflict areas
Article:
GENEVA: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said on Friday in a social media post that urgent support is needed for the health systems of five West Asia countries affected by the conflict — Lebanon, Iran, Iraq, Syria and Jordan, reported Xinhua.
Health systems in the affected countries are "under severe strain" following weeks of intensified violence, Tedros said, adding that the WHO has launched a US$30.3 million flash appeal to support its health response to the escalating conflict in West Asia, with a focus on the five countries.
The appeal covers the period from March to August. It aims to sustain essential health services and trauma care, strengthen disease surveillance and early warning systems, enhance mass casualty management, and improve national readiness for chemical, biological, radiological and nuclear emergencies, Tedros noted.
According to the WHO, as of March 31, the ongoing hostilities in West Asia have triggered the displacement of more than four million people, with over 3,300 deaths and 30,000 injuries. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police arrest two in investigation into Australian fugitive
Article:
MELBOURNE: Australian police said they arrested two people on Saturday as part of an investigation into how a fugitive evaded capture for seven months after he had killed two police officers.
Desmond Freeman was shot and killed by police at a remote property in northern Victoria state on Monday, ending a manhunt which began in August last year when the 56-year-old fled into dense bushland.
Police resources were poured into the search for Freeman after he shot and killed two officers who had come to search his rural home in Victoria.
Victoria Police said a man and woman were arrested on Saturday morning at two separate properties in northeast Victoria and would be interviewed.
"The investigation remains ongoing and as such, we are not in a position to provide further details at this immediate time," the police statement said.
Following the shooting of Freeman, Victoria Police chief commissioner Mike Bush described the manhunt as one of the "most significantly resourced police operations" in Australian history.
Bush said police believed the 56-year-old may have evaded capture with the help of locals.
"It would be very difficult for him to get to where he was without assistance," Bush said on Monday.
Freeman was sought after he killed 59-year-old detective Neal Thompson and 35-year-old senior constable Vadim De Waart.
A third officer was wounded in the lower body.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar urges values-led education, warns against 'excellence without a soul'
Article:
KUALA KANGSAR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called for an education system that balances knowledge with values, warning against producing graduates who are intellectually capable but lack conscience.
Speaking to students at the Malay College Kuala Kangsar (MCKK), he said privilege must come with accountability and moral responsibility.
"There is an important work titled Excellence Without a Soul, which warns of the dangers of pursuing achievement without conscience. This is especially relevant for elite institutions," he said.
"Excellence must be accompanied by awareness and responsibility."
Anwar questioned the purpose of education if it neglects humanity.
"Where is compassion? Where is the dignity of the human spirit? We must be grateful for the opportunities we have, but with privilege comes responsibility, not merely honour," he said.
He added that education must evolve to meet modern demands, including digital technology, artificial intelligence and emerging fields, while ensuring core values are not sidelined.
"We are human beings, not machines. Education must not ignore ethics, values and character," he said.
Highlighting the distinction between information, knowledge and wisdom, Anwar said the modern world is saturated with information, but not all of it leads to understanding.
"There is knowledge that benefits, and there is knowledge that corrupts. We must learn to filter information so that it leads to wisdom," he said.
He warned that without strong moral principles, even advanced knowledge could be misused.
"The world does not lack intelligent people. Yet without conscience, such knowledge can lead to injustice and the destruction of innocent lives," he said, citing global conflicts including Gaza.
Anwar also criticised what he described as double standards among global leaders.
"Why preach human rights but fail to uphold them? This reflects a failure of education that does not instil justice and compassion," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Artemis astronauts more than halfway to Moon, putting Earth in rearview
Article:
HOUSTON: The Artemis 2 astronauts have passed the halfway point between Earth and the Moon on Saturday as they sped toward a planned lunar flyby, with NASA releasing initial images of Earth taken from inside the Orion spacecraft.
Astronaut Christina Koch said the crew had a collective "expression of joy" upon being told of the milestone, which was hit around two days, five hours and 24 minutes after the spacecraft blasted off from the Kennedy Space Center in Florida.
"We can see the Moon out of the docking hatch right now, it is a beautiful sight," Koch said around 11 pm (0400 GMT) Friday, according to the space agency's official live broadcast.
NASA's online dashboard showed early Saturday that the Orion spacecraft carrying the astronauts was more than 229,000 kilometres (142,000 miles) from Earth.
The space agency earlier released images from Orion that included a full portrait of Earth, featuring its deep blue oceans and billowing clouds.
After a flurry of high-stakes activity including a dramatic blast-off and an engine firing that catapulted them on their historic trajectory to circle the Moon, the four astronauts aboard were able to catch their breath, even as they continued to perform a variety of equipment checks and tests.
"There has been a tremendous amount of disbelief for me, it's just so extraordinary," said Canadian Jeremy Hansen during a Q&A session with press late Thursday.
"I really like it up here," said Hansen, on his first ever journey to space. "The views are extraordinary."
"It's really fun to be floating around" in zero gravity, he added. "It just makes me feel like a little kid."
Hansen is on the crew with Americans Koch, Victor Glover and Reid Wiseman.
They are due to loop around the Moon early next week — a feat not accomplished in more than 50 years.
NASA official Lakiesha Hawkins praised the photographs taken by commander Wiseman, calling them "amazing" during a briefing Friday.
"We continue to learn all about our spacecraft as we operate it in deep space with crew for the first time," Hawkins said.
"It's important to remind ourselves of that as we learn a little bit more day by day."
- 'Great spirits' -
Friday's to-do list includes a CPR demonstration and medical kit checks, the US space agency said, as well as preparation for the scientific observations they'll need to document when they are closest to the Moon on day six of their journey.
NASA officials reported Friday that all systems were performing well, and that the astronauts were in "great spirits" and had spoken to their families.
The next major milestone of the approximately 10-day journey is expected overnight Sunday into Monday, at which point the astronauts will enter the "lunar sphere of influence" — when the Moon's gravity will have stronger pull on the spacecraft than Earth's.
If all proceeds smoothly, as Orion whips around the Moon the astronauts could set a record by venturing farther from Earth than any human before.
"There is nothing normal about this," said mission commander Wiseman late Thursday.
"Sending four humans 250,000 miles away is a Herculean effort, and we are now just realizing the gravity of that."
The Artemis 2 mission is part of a longer-term plan to repeatedly return to the Moon, with the goal of establishing a permanent lunar base that will offer a platform for further exploration. — AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jertih home fire leaves family with severe burns, neighbour among first responders
Article:
JERTIH, April 4 — A mother and her son suffered burns to almost half of their bodies while trying to escape an early morning fire at their home in Kampung Oh here.
Jertih Fire and Rescue Station chief Mustafa Ismail said both victims, identified as Fauziah Ali, 75, and her son Azli Abu, 45, were sent by members of the public to the Besut Hospital for treatment.
“Our team received a call at 5.14 am, following which a fire engine along with eight personnel were dispatched to the location.
“Upon arriving at the scene, it was found that the fire involved a semi-permanent house, occupied by two family members, which was 80 per cent destroyed,” he said in a statement today.
Mustafa said both victims were confirmed to have suffered 45 per cent burn injuries by the medical authorities.
He said the cause of the fire and the estimated losses are still under investigation, and the extinguishing operation was fully completed by 7.28 am. Meanwhile, the victims’ neighbour, Mohd Rafain Ruzlan, 30, said he heard Azli calling for help around 5 am.
“I happened to be awake at that time and was outside the house, so I rushed over and had to kick the main door open since both victims took time to open the door.
“When I entered, the fire, which was believed to have started from inside a room, had burned the mattress and was spreading. Azli was trying to extinguish the fire.
“I asked the victims to get out to save themselves, but I had to carry Fauziah out of the house. However, both of them were injured, with Azli crawling out of the house to save himself,” he said when met at the scene. — Bernama

Title: Naked man disrupting traffic in Langkap detained for psychiatric evaluation
Article:
IPOH, April 4 — Police detained a man who was behaving erratically and disrupting traffic in the town of Langkap, near Teluk Intan, yesterday.
Hilir Perak police chief ACP Chua Kok Lian said a report was received at 9.35 pm about a naked man acting indecently and obstructing traffic along Jalan Langkap–Chui Chak.
“Acting on the information, police detained the 41-year-old man as seen in videos circulating on social media.
“The suspect was taken to the Langkap police station for further action before being referred to Teluk Intan Hospital for treatment and psychiatric evaluation,” he said in a statement today.
Chua also advised the public not to share videos of the incident to protect the dignity of the individual involved. —Bernama

Title: Terengganu work-from-home plan still under evaluation, no decision yet
Article:
KUALA NERUS, April 4 — The Terengganu State Government is assessing the need to implement a work from home (WFH) arrangement for its civil servants in light of the global energy crisis arising from conflicts in West Asia.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri  Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar said the proposal is still under evaluation, and no decision has been finalised.
“The matter has yet to be brought to a meeting and is still being assessed,” he told reporters after launching the fifth Ijtimak Antarabangsa Perpaduan Ummah and Himpunan Alam Melayu 2026 programme here today.
He said the state government is carefully considering the need for such an arrangement, taking into account differing conditions between states.
“The situation in the Klang Valley is not the same as in Terengganu. In the Klang Valley, people spend hours on the road, which affects fuel consumption. This is not the case in Terengganu, but we will continue to assess whether there is a need to implement a work from home arrangement,” he said.
Meanwhile, Ahmad Samsuri said a recent special meeting with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim was aimed at sharing the latest economic developments and measures to address current pressures.
“We have a role at the state level in addressing the current economic pressures, and steps will be taken accordingly, although I will not go into details today.
“We are still assessing the impact, particularly on the state administration, and will mobilise cooperation from all parties, including the government, private sector and federal government, to address the economic challenges faced by the people. Such collective efforts are essential to ease the pressure,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Residents fear black panther after sheep carcass found near Gua Musang Hospital quarters
Article:
GUA MUSANG, April 4 — About 30 families living in the Gua Musang Hospital quarters are worried about their safety after a carcass of a sheep, belonging to a resident, was found 10 metres from their premises on Thursday.
They suspect a black panther was responsible for the attack.
One of the residents, Mohd Helmiey Shudi Hussin, 42, said that he had been living in the quarters for six years, and this incident was a first.
“The incident occurred very close to the quarters, giving rise to fear and concern for our safety, especially for the children who play outside.
“I understand that some residents who have lived here much longer have encountered a panther in the hospital quarters area…but it was caught a few years ago,” he said when met by reporters here today.
Mohd Helmiey hoped that immediate action would be taken by the authorities, adding that the quarters are close to a cave that may be the habitat of the wild animal.
Meanwhile, Kelantan Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) director Wan Mohd Adib Wan Yusoh, when contacted, said that his department would obtain further information regarding the incident. — Bernama

Title: Anwar says large projects on hold amid rising costs, but rakyat-focused initiatives will continue
Article:
IPOH, April 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim says several large development projects will be postponed due to rising costs caused by the ongoing conflict in West Asia, but projects directly benefiting the rakyat will continue.
“These include repairing school toilets, fixing school facilities, mending leaking mosque roofs and improving houses for Orang Asli communities. We will consider what we can afford, but large projects will be postponed,” he said during his speech at the Majlis Jalinan Mesra Madani at Masjid Ar-Rashid, Taman Perpaduan Koperasi Tambun here.
Anwar also instructed ministers to temporarily refrain from overseas travel, citing soaring costs.
“Previously, a flight from Kuala Lumpur to London cost about RM7,000. Now it can reach RM20,000,” he said.
He added that the prime minister of New Zealand had informed him that about 1,000 flights had been cancelled due to fuel shortages.
“The Prime Minister also said that even when fuel is available, there is no guarantee flights can proceed because aircraft may need to stop at multiple airports to refuel,” Anwar said.
Acknowledging that prices of goods may rise, he said the government is assisting farmers and fishermen with fuel subsidies and working on measures to gradually address diesel price increases.
“We are also studying the situation with businesses and suppliers. The National Economic Action Council holds daily meetings, and we have even invited Tan Sri Hassan Marican, the former head of Petronas, to provide advice,” he said.
Anwar said Cabinet meetings are now held more frequently to address economic challenges.
“Today I chair the meeting, tomorrow it will be chaired by Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, and the day after by Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. We listen to the problems faced by farmers, planters and businesses. Fertiliser prices are rising because we import it. Chicken feed prices are also increasing. When costs go up, the prices of goods may also rise,” he said.
He added that the government hopes the conflict will end soon to stabilise the global economic situation.
“That is why we pray that this war will end soon,” he said.

Title: Anwar urges Malaysians to stay grateful, united amid economic growth and global pressures
Article:
IPOH, April 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Malaysians to remain grateful and united as the country continues to post strong economic growth despite global uncertainties.
“Just last week, Bank Negara announced that our economic growth at the end of last year and early this year was among the best. Investments are the highest in history. Inflation is very low. Unemployment is very low,” he said.
Anwar made the remarks during his speech at the Majlis Jalinan Mesra Madani at Masjid Ar-Rashid, Taman Perpaduan Koperasi Tambun here.
He credited the achievements to cooperation across all sectors, including the federal and state governments, civil servants, the private sector, workers and the public.
“These are our achievements, thanks to the cooperation of everyone — the federal government, state governments, civil servants, private companies, workers and the public. Through unity, we have strengthened the economy,” he said.
However, Anwar warned that external pressures, particularly the war in the Gulf region, have increased government spending by about RM6 billion per month.
“The conflict has also raised questions about Malaysia’s oil production. The chairman of Petronas has already explained that while we do produce oil, we also import it. Yes, we sell our oil, but we have to import at very high prices — about USD106 per barrel. And that price does not even include insurance, shipping and transportation costs, which are all increasing,” he said.
Even so, he said Malaysia remains fortunate compared with some neighbouring countries.
“Alhamdulillah, we still have oil. Try looking at neighbouring countries. Some are already using fuel rationing because they do not have enough oil.
“In one Asean country, 400 petrol stations have closed. In another country’s capital city, people are walking to their offices because there is no fuel,” he said.
Anwar noted that Malaysians are not facing long queues for petrol and should appreciate the situation.
“We should be grateful that Malaysia has not reached that stage. So do not keep complaining endlessly. I am used to being criticised or insulted; that is fine. But in this situation, we must work together and find solutions,” he said.
He added that Malaysia remains one of the few peaceful countries with a growing economy and strong inter-communal relations, including Malays, Chinese, Indians, Orang Asli, Dayaks and Kadazans.
“Our real challenges are outside. Malaysia remains one of the few peaceful countries with a growing economy. Our country is peaceful, and relations between the races are still good. If we are grateful, our blessings will increase.
“If we are ungrateful, the punishment will be severe. So we must be thankful, make efforts to improve and avoid fighting among ourselves,” he said.

Title: Anwar: State leaders entitled to salaries, my decision not to take pay is personal choice
Article:
IPOH, April 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said it is incorrect to argue that Menteri Besar, ministers or other leaders should forgo their salaries simply because he has chosen not to take his own since assuming office.
“Some people say, ‘If the Prime Minister does not take his salary, why should a Menteri Besar take theirs?’ That argument is not correct. Their salaries are their rights,” he said.
Anwar made the remarks during his speech at the Majlis Jalinan Mesra Madani at Masjid Ar-Rashid, Taman Perpaduan Koperasi Tambun here.
He stressed that the government’s focus is not on denying legitimate salaries, but on controlling excessive spending, especially amid current economic challenges.
“When it comes to the economy, we must be careful and avoid wasteful spending. That is why the Prime Minister’s open house was not held this year. What we are trying to control is excessive spending. That is the point I want to emphasise,” he said.
Anwar added that he has not taken his salary for three years since becoming Prime Minister.
“I thought of taking it again when the economy improved, but now the economy is facing difficulties, so I decided not to take it yet,” he said.
He said the decision does not cause him hardship as his official travel and car are provided by the government.
“That is alright. I am not poor. My travel and official car are provided by the government, so I do not face hardship,” he said.
However, he said he wanted to set an example to his colleagues about the importance of prudence in government spending.
“But I must also show my colleagues the importance of being prudent. Of course, other people must still receive their salaries because that is their right,” he added.

Title: Wee Ka Siong pays tribute to Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik, calls him towering statesman and nation builder
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — MCA president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong today paid tribute to the late Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik, describing him as a towering statesman, nation builder, and extraordinary son of Malaysia.
In a Facebook post, Wee said Dr Ling’s life was defined by resilience, intellect, and an unwavering sense of duty, noting that the former MCA president had devoted his life to public service and nation building.
“Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik was not merely a leader, he was a nation builder, a unifier, and a visionary whose legacy will live on through the institutions he built, the policies he shaped, and the lives he touched,” he said.
Wee said Dr Ling, who was born in Kuala Kangsar, Perak in 1943, was raised by his mother after the early loss of his father, an experience that shaped his compassion and commitment to serving the people.
Trained as a medical doctor, Dr Ling made the decision to leave the profession to enter politics, believing public service could uplift millions of lives, Wee added.
He noted that Dr Ling rose to become MCA’s sixth president during a challenging political and economic period, steering the party with calm leadership and strengthening unity within the Chinese community and the broader national framework.
Dr Ling served as the Member of Parliament for Mata Kuching from 1974 to 1986 before representing Labis for four terms from 1986 to 2004, while also holding key positions including deputy information minister, deputy finance minister and deputy education minister before being appointed transport minister in 1986.
Wee said Dr Ling’s 17-year tenure as transport minister played a key role in modernising Malaysia’s aviation, port and rail infrastructure, helping to position the country as a globally connected economy.
He added that Dr Ling’s most enduring legacy was in education, citing his role in expanding Tunku Abdul Rahman College, now known as Tunku Abdul Rahman University of Management and Technology, and realising the long-cherished goal of establishing Universiti Tunku Abdul Rahman (UTAR), which was approved in 2001 and launched in 2002.
Wee said Dr Ling remained committed to education even after retiring from politics, continuing to contribute to the development of institutions and opportunities for future generations.
“On behalf of MCA, I extend my deepest condolences to his family and loved ones. May they find strength and comfort in this difficult time,” he said.
Wee also announced that MCA flags at all party premises nationwide would be flown at half-mast as a mark of respect and to commemorate Dr Ling’s legacy.

Title: Penang LRT hits milestone with first concrete segment cast in Kulim
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, April 4 — The Penang Mutiara Line LRT project has achieved a significant milestone with the segmental box girder (SBG) casting yard in Sungai Ular, Kulim now 90 per cent complete.
Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said in a statement today that the 15.378-hectare facility is set to play a critical role in constructing the project’s elevated guideway by producing the concrete SBG segments that form its main structure.
Chow, who visited the site this morning, expressed satisfaction with the steady progress, saying it reflects growing momentum in delivering the state’s first LRT system.
He added that the casting yard is an important step as the project moves into its next phase, with full completion of the facility expected by May 2026 following the start of works in April 2025.
SRS LRT project director Adil Putra Ahmad said the facility will produce approximately 8,800 SBG segments required for the Civil Main Contractor 1 (CMC1) package, which covers a 23.7-kilometre alignment from Silicon Island to Komtar.
Supported by a workforce of 400 personnel and 20 mould sets, the yard is expected to produce up to 350 segments per month at peak operations, with the first segment already successfully cast.
The statement said the segmental construction method was chosen for its suitability to Penang’s urban environment, as it improves efficiency while minimising ground-level disruptions and enhancing safety.
Piling and pier construction are currently underway, and the first spans, each measuring 40 metres long and consisting of 13 segments, are expected to be launched at sites like Bandar Seri Pinang between July and August.
The Chief Minister advised the public that while SBG launching is typically carried out at night in busy areas to minimise traffic impact, there may be necessary adjustments to traffic management plans (TMP) as construction activities increase.
He also emphasised that with continued coordination and prompt approvals, the state government remains confident the project is on track for its targeted commencement of operations in 2031. — Bernama

Title: King and Queen express condolences on passing of Ling Liong Sik
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, King and Queen of Malaysia have expressed their condolences on the passing of former Transport Minister and MCA Life Honorary President Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik, who died this morning.
The message of condolence was conveyed via the official Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar.
Dr Ling, 83, who was also a former MCA president, was among the longest-serving veteran politicians in the Cabinet, including holding the Transport Ministry portfolio, in addition to leading the MCA for 17 years.
The late Dr Ling served as the Member of Parliament for Mata Kuching for three terms from 1974 to 1982, and as the Member of Parliament for Labis for four terms from 1986 to 1999.
During that period, he held various key positions in the government, including Parliamentary Secretary, Deputy Information Minister, Deputy Finance Minister, and Deputy Education Minister.
His final post was as Transport Minister, a position he held for 14 years from 1986 until his resignation in 2003. — Bernama

Title: Fahmi Fadzil leads tributes as ex-MCA president Ling Liong Sik dies
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik’s dedication, leadership and decades of service in government will be long remembered and continue to inspire future generations, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said.
In a Facebook post today, Fahmi extended his condolences to Toh Puan Ong Ee Nah and the family of the former MCA president, describing his passing as a great loss to the nation.
Dr Ling, who was also MCA life honorary president, died this morning aged 83.
Fahmi said Dr Ling made significant contributions to Malaysia’s development throughout his career, including serving as Deputy Information Minister from 1976 to 1982.
“May Toh Puan and the family be granted strength and fortitude in facing this loss. May he rest in peace,” he said.
Dr Ling was among the country’s longest-serving politicians, having served as Transport Minister from 1986 to 2003 and led MCA for 17 years. — Bernama

Title: Anwar calls for unity over political point-scoring as global energy crunch bites
Article:
KUALA KANGSAR, April 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said he is open to discussions with the opposition and any parties willing to help the government find solutions to address the current global energy crisis.
He stressed that any criticism or comments regarding the country’s economic management, especially those involving rising global oil prices, should be based on knowledge and accurate facts, rather than mere empty criticism.
“The economic crisis is is beyond our control, although at times the opposition’s rhetoric may sound sophisticated.
